zh.lifeSummary: Lady, Elizabeth, M. Lockhart of Cleghorn於1560年出生在英國蘇格蘭藍納可爾可萊。她於大約1576年與Lord, Alan Lockhart VI of Ceghorn在英國蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是9個兒子的父母。她去世於1594年7月16日在她的家鄉享年34歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lady, Elizabeth, M. Lockhart of Cleghorn于1560年出生在英国苏格兰藍納可爾可萊。她于大约1576年与Lord, Alan Lockhart VI of Ceghorn在英国苏格兰结婚。他们至少是9个儿子的父母。她去世于1594年7月16日在她的家乡享年34岁。

nameMeanings.name: Lockhart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English: nickname from Old French locart, a Normanno-Picard form of Old French lo(s)chart ‘one who squints, who is cross-eyed’, attested in 13th- and 14th-century Arras (Pas-de-Calais) as Louchard, Lochard, Locard and Locart. It is a derivative of Latin luscus ‘one-eyed’. In France Locard is mostly found in Normandy (especially Calvados) c. 1900, while Louchart is mainly concentrated in Pas-de-Calais at the same time.
Americanized form of German   Luckhardt  .

History: According to family tradition, Sir Simon Locard held the key to the locked silver casket in which Robert the Bruce's heart was taken to be buried in the Holy Land in 1329. This unverifiable story is no doubt related to the later spelling of the Scottish surname as Lockhart and to the addition of a heart and fetterlock to the family coat of arms (date unknown).

variants.name: Lockard
meanings: Scottish and English: variant of   Lockhart   1 and 2.

variants.name: Lockart
meanings: Scottish and English: variant of   Lockhart  .

variants.name: Lockheart
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Lockhart  .

variants.name: Lockert
meanings: German: variant of   Lockhart   3.

variants.name: Luckhardt
meanings: German: from the ancient Germanic female personal name Liutgard, a compound of liud ‘people, tribe’ + gard ‘protective enclosure, yard’. Liutgard was a popular name in medieval times; among others it was the name of the daughter of emperor Otto I and the wife of Charlemagne.

variants.name: Lochard
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Lockhart  .
French: from a derivative of Old French loche ‘loach’, later also ‘species of giant slug’, hence, in the latter case, a nickname for a slow man. This surname is also found in Haiti.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Cleghorn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Central and southeastScotland, North eastern England): habitational name from a place in Lanarkshire called Cleghorn.

variants.name: Claghorn
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Cleghorn  .
History: James Claghorn was shipped to New England in 1651 as a prisoner,having been one of the Scottish opponents of Oliver Cromwell. In 1662he is recorded as a freeman of Barnstable, MA.

variants.name: Gleghorn
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Cleghorn  .

nameMeanings.name: Lady,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Polish: variant of   Lada  .
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: Lady, Elizabeth, M. Lockhart of Cleghorn was born in 1560, in Cleghorn, Lanarkshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. She married Lord, Alan Lockhart VI of Ceghorn about 1576, in Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 9 sons. She died on 16 July 1594, in her hometown, at the age of 34.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady, Elizabeth, M. Lockhart of Cleghorn est née en 1560, à Cleghorn, Lanarkshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé Lord, Alan Lockhart VI of Ceghorn environ 1576, à Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 9 fils. Elle est décédée le 16 juillet 1594, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 34 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Lady, Elizabeth, M. Lockhart of Cleghorn wurde 1560 in Cleghorn, Lanarkshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hat um 1576 in Schottland, United Kingdom Lord, Alan Lockhart VI of Ceghorn geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 9 Söhne. Sie starb am 16. Juli 1594 im Alter von 34 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Lady, Elizabeth, M. Lockhart of Cleghorn è nata il nel 1560 nel Cleghorn, Lanarkshire, Scozia, Regno Unito. Lei sposato Lord, Alan Lockhart VI of Ceghorn circa 1576, nel Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 9 figli. Morì il 16 luglio 1594, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 34 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady, Elizabeth, M. Lockhart of Cleghornは1560年にイギリススコットランドラナルクシュアイアクレグホールンで生まれた。約1576年にイギリススコットランドでLord, Alan Lockhart VI of Ceghornと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも9人の息子の親であった。彼女は1594年7月16日に故郷で34歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady, Elizabeth, M. Lockhart of Cleghorn(은)는 1560년 영국 스코틀랜드 랜아르크실르 클레고른에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1576년 영국 스코틀랜드에서 Lord, Alan Lockhart VI of Ceghorn(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1594년 7월 16일 그녀의 고향에서 34세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Lady, Elizabeth, M. Lockhart of Cleghorn nasceu em 1560, em Cleghorn, Lanarkshire, Escócia, United Kingdom. Ela casou-se com Lord, Alan Lockhart VI of Ceghorn aproximadamente 1576, em Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 9 filhos. Ela faleceu em 16 de julho de 1594, em sua cidade natal, com 34 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Lady, Elizabeth, M. Lockhart of Cleghorn родилась в 1560 г. в местности клегаурн, ланаркшир, Шотландия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Lord, Alan Lockhart VI of Ceghorn около 1576 г. в местности Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей. Она умерла 16 июля 1594 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 34 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Lady, Elizabeth, M. Lockhart of Cleghorn nació en 1560, en Cleghorn, Lanarkshire, Escocia, Reino Unido. Se casó con Lord, Alan Lockhart VI of Ceghorn aproximadamente 1576, en Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 9 hijos. Murió el 16 de julio de 1594, en su pueblo, a la edad de 34 años.
